markdown:
#  Export the Drawings Log to a PDF or CSV 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Read Only' or higher permissions on the project's Drawings tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * You can narrow down the list of drawings included in the export by entering a search term or filtering by a discipline. See [Search and Filter the Drawings Log](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/search-for-and-filter-drawings).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawings** tool.  
_Note:_ If your project uses [drawing areas](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-drawing-areas), open the area you want to export drawings from.
  2. Click **Export** and select a **PDF** or **CSV** file type.
  3. The download will begin immediately and include the following information:
     * **Discipline** : The discipline the drawing is listed under. In the PDF, this appears as a gray header above each discipline.
     * **Number** : The number that appears on each drawing.
     * **Title** : The title that appears on the drawing.
     * **Revision** : The drawing's revision number.
     * **Drawing Authored** : The date the drawings were authored.
     * **Received Received** : The date a drawing was received or issued by its author.
     * **Set** : The set the drawing was uploaded into.


## See Also
  * [Drawings - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/permissions)
  * [Which tools can I extract project data from using the Procore Extracts application?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-tools-can-i-extract-project-data-from-using-the-procore-extracts-application)
  * [View the Deleted Drawing Revisions Report](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/view-the-deleted-drawing-revisions-report)
title: Export the Drawings Log to a PDF or CSV
description: How to export the drawings log in Procore to a PDF or CSV file on your computer.
